question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload signature files that cause `signatureFileReader.read(s)` to return `StreamFileSignature` objects with null `getFilename()`, causing a `NullPointerException` when `map.put(s.getFilename(), s)` is called in the collect step, which propagates as an unhandled error and crashes the entire download batch, halting transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload a data file that causes `verify(streamFile, signature)` to throw `HashMismatchException` for every node that has `CONSENSUS_REACHED` status, causing all nodes to be exhausted and `verifySignatures()` to return false, permanently stalling the processing of that transaction file without any retry mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload a stream file where `streamFile.copy().clear()` produces a copy with a manipulated `getHash()` value, causing `lastStreamFile` to be updated with an incorrect hash anchor, so that the next legitimate transaction file fails `verifyHashChain()` and is rejected, creating a persistent denial-of-service against transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload signature files with crafted content that causes `signatureFileReader.read(s)` to produce `StreamFileSignature` objects with identical `filename` and `node` values as legitimate signatures, causing duplicate entries in the `TreeMultimap` that inflate the apparent consensus count and allow tampered transaction files to pass the 1/3 threshold check?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload signature files for a filename that sorts before all legitimate files in the `TreeMultimap`, causing `earliestFilename` to be set to the attacker-controlled filename, and then when `verifySignatures()` fails, the warning log message reveals internal state (previous file name, node IDs) that aids further targeted attacks against the transaction verification pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload a data file that passes `verify()` but whose `streamFileNotifier.verified(streamFile)` call in `onVerified()` triggers downstream processing of tampered transaction data, given that by the time `onVerified()` is called the file has already passed all hash and signature checks based on attacker-controlled signature files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload an extremely large signature file that exceeds memory limits when parsed by `signatureFileReader.read(s)`, causing an `OutOfMemoryError` that is not caught by `onErrorContinue` (which only catches `Throwable` subclasses that are `Exception`), crashing the JVM and halting all transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStartAfterFilename()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage cause `dateRangeCalculator.getLastStreamFile(streamType)` to return a stream file with a manipulated `getName()` value, causing `getStartAfterFilename()` to return a `StreamFilename` that skips over a range of legitimate transaction files, creating gaps in the mirror node's transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload a data file where `signature.getDataFilename()` resolves to a path that causes `streamFileProvider.get(dataFilename)` to fetch a different file than intended (e.g., via path traversal in the filename), allowing the attacker to substitute a tampered transaction file in place of the legitimate one?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload signature files with crafted `StreamFilename` values that cause `Ordering.natural()` comparison in the `TreeMultimap` to produce an unexpected ordering, causing tampered transaction files to be processed in a sequence that bypasses the hash chain verification by exploiting the order-dependent `lastStreamFile` state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload a data file that causes `Utility.archiveFile()` (called when `downloaderProperties.isWriteFiles()` is true) to write attacker-controlled bytes to an attacker-influenced `archiveDestinationFolder` path, potentially overwriting legitimate archived transaction files on disk?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload signature files such that `signatures.forEach(s -> Utility.archiveFile(s.getFilename().getBucketFilePath(), s.getBytes(), archiveDestinationFolder))` (when `isWriteSignatures()` is true) writes attacker-controlled signature bytes to disk, potentially poisoning the archived signature store used for audit purposes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the scenario where `allNodes.size() <= 1` causes `partialCollection()` to return the entire node list without shuffling, and if only one node is configured, the entire consensus check relies on a single node's signature, making it trivial to achieve false consensus on tampered transaction files by controlling that single node's cloud storage path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload signature files that cause `statusMap(signatures, nodeIds)` to be called with a `signatures` collection containing `StreamFileSignature` objects with null `getNode()`, causing a `NullPointerException` in `s.getNode().getNodeId()` that propagates as an unhandled exception and crashes the verification loop?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload signature files that cause `signatureFileReader.read(s)` to return `StreamFileSignature` objects with a null `getStreamType()`, causing a `NullPointerException` when `streamFileSignature.setStreamType(streamType)` is called or when `getStreamType().toString()` is called in `statusMap()`, crashing the download pipeline?
